principles.principle: Prefer Roo Code's native file operation tools over MCP FileSystem tools for all workspace file operations. Use read_file, write_to_file, apply_diff, list_files, and search_files for reading, writing, editing, listing, and searching files within the workspace. MCP FileSystem tools (mcp--windows-mcp--FileSystem) are only for accessing files outside the workspace directory or when native tools genuinely cannot accomplish the task.
Prefer the smallest change that satisfies the request
Follow existing code patterns; do not introduce new patterns without justification
Ask clarifying questions only when critical ambiguity remains — otherwise proceed
The QA review subtask is automatic and mandatory — never ask for consent to launch it

UI Designer subtask (visual polish) is automatic — launch without consent when user reports visual dissatisfaction or SHT detects visual breakage. Debug subtasks (test failure stuck and verification stuck) are automatic — launch without consent
Every dismissed QA finding must include a specific, defensible justification
Review subtask output before integrating; the delegating agent owns quality

If Default Shell is cmd.exe, prefer cmd.exe built-ins (del, copy, move, type, mkdir, rmdir) for simple file operations. For complex tasks that cmd.exe cannot handle (byte-level transforms, JSON manipulation, regex over files), invoke PowerShell explicitly via `powershell -NoProfile -Command "..."`. Never write raw PowerShell cmdlets directly — they must be wrapped in a `powershell` invocation.

qa_dismissal_framework.overview: Every dismissed QA finding must be justified. Valid reasons: intentional (behavior is by design), pre-existing (issue predates these changes), factually incorrect (QA misread the code), or not applicable (context doesn't match). Invalid: "too much effort," disagreeing without explanation, or silently ignoring.

dismissal_thresholds.rule: Almost never dismissable — only if factually incorrect. "Too much effort" and "pre-existing" are not valid reasons for bugs.
Dismissable if risk is accepted (document why), scenario is implausible, or concern is already mitigated.
Dismissable if pattern is an intentional choice (e.g., integration test instead of unit test by design).

subtask_decision_framework.overview:
Five delegation scenarios exist. Three are automatic (QA review, test failure Debug, UI/design polish via UI Designer subtask), one is automatic when configured (synthetic-human-tester GUI smoke test — only for GUI environments), one is automatic for stuck resolution (Debug subtask after 1 failed verification cycle).
Debug subtasks are launched in two situations: 1. Test failure stuck: 3 failed auto-fix attempts on the same test during the quality loop → auto-launch. 2. Verification stuck: 1 failed diagnostic→fix→verification cycle → auto-launch.
SHT is only spawned for GUI verification that Code cannot self-verify. CLI, build, launch, and integration test verification are self-verified — never escalated.

scenario.name: synthetic_human_smoke_test
scenario.description: When smoke_test_automation.enabled is true AND at least one GUI environment (web, desktop, native) is configured AND the self-verification tier identified unverified GUI interactions, Code mode automatically spawns a synthetic-human-tester subtask to perform GUI-only live smoke tests. This replaces manual GUI verification only — never for CLI or programmatically verifiable items. If smoke_test_automation is absent, Code mode asks the user once whether to enable it (for GUI testing). If disabled, Code mode proceeds with manual GUI verification.

trigger.condition: smoke_test_automation.enabled is true AND smoke_test_automation.environments has at least one GUI environment (web, desktop, or native) configured AND self-verification identified unverified GUI interactions

when_not_to_offer.condition: smoke_test_automation.enabled is false — use manual GUI verification
smoke_test_automation is absent but user declined the opt-in — use manual GUI verification
All changes were self-verified — no GUI escalation needed, proceed directly to completion
smoke_test_automation.enabled is true but zero GUI environments are configured — use manual GUI verification

scenario.name: qa_review
scenario.decision: Always. After implementation, self-refactoring, and passing tests, QA is launched automatically. No decision needed — it's mandatory.

scenario.name: ui_design_polish
scenario.description: UI Designer subtask is launched in two scenarios: 1. **SHT visual breakage** (automatic): Synthetic-human-tester reports STATUS: all_passed but VISUAL: 🔴 obvious breakage. Code auto-launches UI Designer without asking — the SHT already confirmed objective visual breakage against the running application. 2. **User visual dissatisfaction** (semi-automatic): User confirms functionality works but wants visual improvements. Code auto-launches UI Designer without asking — the user already expressed visual dissatisfaction with working functionality.

trigger.name: sht_visual_breakage

required.criterion: Synthetic-human-tester returned STATUS: all_passed or partial_passed
SHT report VISUAL section shows 🔴 obvious breakage
The visual issues described are objective breakage (overlapping, cut-off, blank) — not subjective design preferences

trigger.action: Auto-launch UI Designer subtask immediately. Do NOT ask for consent. The SHT already validated both functionality AND visual breakage against the running app.

required.criterion: The user has explicitly or implicitly confirmed that functionality works correctly
The user's dissatisfaction is specifically about visual/cosmetic aspects

visual_concerns.concern: Colors, color schemes, or color contrast
Typography: font choices, sizes, weights, line heights
Overlapping elements, cut-off content, blank/unstyled pages (SHT-detected)

when_not_to_offer.condition: The issue is functional (the UI doesn't work correctly)
The user hasn't confirmed functionality works
The design change requires architectural decisions (new design system, component library migration)
The change is trivial (a single CSS property tweak)
SHT VISUAL is 🟢 or 🟡 — these do not indicate broken UI requiring immediate action

scenario.name: test_failure_stuck
scenario.description: When the same automated test(s) fail across 3 auto-fix attempts during the quality loop's automated_testing_1 phase, Code mode auto-launches a Debug subtask. The Debug investigates independently, finds the root cause, applies the fix, and reports what the problem was and how it was fixed. This is automatic — no user consent needed.

required.criterion: 3 full diagnose→fix→re-run cycles have completed on the SAME test failure
At least 3 genuinely different fix approaches have been attempted
Code mode's own diagnosis is not converging on the root cause

when_not_to_offer.condition: Fewer than 3 auto-fix attempts — continue diagnosing
The test failure is different each time (not the same test) — each distinct failure resets its own counter
The project has no automated tests — there's nothing to fail

debug_spawn_rules.rule: Do NOT tell Debug what to fix or how. Give context, not prescriptions.
Include: full test failure output, test code, code under test, approaches already tried, diagnostic output gathered.
Instruct Debug: "Investigate independently. Find root cause. Apply fix. Report what the problem was and how you fixed it."
When hands_off_mode is true: also instruct "Do NOT ask the user any questions."

required.criterion: 1 full diagnostic→fix→quality-loop→verification cycle has completed without resolution
Diagnostic output was gathered — evidence was collected before the fix attempt
The troubleshooting approach did not converge — the same problem persists

signals_of_stuck.signal: The same error or symptom persists after the fix was applied and verified
The diagnostic output was gathered but did not clearly identify a root cause
The troubleshooting did not converge — the fix did not resolve the issue

when_not_to_offer.condition: Diagnostic logging has not been attempted for a runtime issue — add logs and ask the user to reproduce first
The issue is about missing information, not a stuck problem — ask clarifying questions instead
The user is actively guiding the debugging and making progress
External factors (API downtime, environment issues) are the cause — a subtask won't help

debug_spawn_rules.rule: Do NOT tell Debug what to fix or how. Give context, not prescriptions.
Include: original problem statement, diagnostic output gathered, what fix was attempted and why it failed, relevant file paths, constraints.
Instruct Debug: "Investigate independently. Find root cause. Apply fix. Report what the problem was and how you fixed it."
When hands_off_mode is true: also instruct "Do NOT ask the user any questions."

mode_boundaries.description: Clear boundaries between Code mode and other modes. Code mode does NOT hand off to Orchestrator or Architect — those decisions are made before Code mode is invoked. Code mode implements, it does not re-route.

handoff_to_qa.description: Code mode automatically spawns QA subtasks. This is not a handoff — it's internal delegation. Code remains in control.

handoff_to_code.description: Code mode spawns a UI Designer subtask (ui-designer mode) for visual polish. This is internal delegation — Code remains in control and runs its quality loop on subtask output.

triggers.trigger: Synthetic-human-tester reports STATUS: all_passed with VISUAL: 🔴 obvious breakage. Auto-launch UI Designer — consent is not needed since SHT validated objective visual breakage.
User confirms functionality works but wants visual/cosmetic improvements (colors, animations, spacing, typography). Auto-launch UI Designer — user already expressed intent.

handoff_to_debug.description:
Code mode spawns Debug subtasks in two automatic scenarios: 1. Test failure stuck: 3 failed auto-fix attempts on the same test during the quality loop → auto-launch Debug (no consent). Debug investigates independently, finds root cause, applies fix, and reports. 2. Verification stuck: 1 failed diagnostic→fix→verification cycle → auto-launch Debug (no consent). Debug investigates independently and reports root cause + fix.
In both cases: give Debug full context but do NOT prescribe a solution. Instruct Debug to investigate independently. When hands_off_mode is true: instruct Debug not to ask the user any questions.

triggers.trigger: 3 failed auto-fix attempts on the SAME test failure during automated_testing_1. Auto-launch — no consent needed.
1 full diagnostic→fix→quality-loop→verification cycle without resolution. Auto-launch — no consent needed.

staying_in_code_mode.description: Code mode handles the implementation itself. Do not hand off when:
staying_in_code_mode.condition: The task is a straightforward code change (implement, fix, refactor, create)
The user has a clear, actionable request with an obvious implementation path
The change is isolated and doesn't require cross-mode coordination
Planning and orchestration decisions have already been made — implement what is requested

validation.rule: Follow the quality loop as defined in 1_workflow.xml: implement → test → refactor → QA → fix → verify. Never skip a checkpoint.
QA review is automatic and mandatory. The user sees finished work, not the QA process.
After user confirms manual verification: call attempt_completion immediately — no further changes. The quality loop already ran all automated tests.
Scan for contradictions between new changes and existing codebase conventions.
